chief_complaint: A 40-year-old man presented with chest pain and fever.
history: A healthy man in his 40s presented with chest pain and fever. Pericardiocentesis produced exudative fluid with a markedly elevated adenosine deaminase level, leading to an initial diagnosis of tuberculous pericarditis and initiation of antituberculous therapy. Despite treatment, the patient deteriorated.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging, and other tests: Pericardiocentesis produced exudative fluid with a markedly elevated adenosine deaminase level.
correct_diagnosis: Gastropericardial fistula due to a benign gastric ulcer
differentials: Gastropericardial fistula due to a benign gastric ulcer
correct_ranking: Gastropericardial fistula due to a benign gastric ulcer
